title: Cardiac Patient — Visit 2023-10-20
text: Patient is a 50-year-old female presenting with chest pain radiating to the left arm. History of hypertension and hyperlipidemia. Current medications include atorvastatin 40mg and metoprolol 50mg. ECG shows ST elevation. Plan is to admit to CCU and schedule for urgent cardiac catheterization. Vitals: Temp 98.7F, HR 81, BP 158/94, RR 14, SpO2 98% on RA. General: Alert and oriented x 3, in no acute distress. HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic, PERRLA. Neck: Supple, no JVD or lymphadenopathy. Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm, normal S1 and S2, no murmurs, rubs, or gallops. Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally, no wheezes, rales, or rhonchi, except as noted in HPI. Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended, bowel sounds active in all 4 quadrants. Extremities: No clubbing or cyanosis. Pulses 2+ bilaterally. Skin: Warm and dry, no rashes or lesions. Neurological: Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Motor and sensory exams unremarkable. Assessment and Plan: As detailed above. Will follow up in 2 weeks or sooner if symptoms worsen.

title: Cardiac Patient — Visit 2024-10-10
text: Patient presents with worsening shortness of breath and pitting edema in both lower extremities. Echocardiogram reveals an ejection fraction of 35%. Patient has a history of congestive heart failure. Added furosemide 40mg daily and advised strict fluid restriction. Vitals: Temp 98.5F, HR 93, BP 118/85, RR 21, SpO2 93% on RA. General: Alert and oriented x 3, in no acute distress. HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic, PERRLA. Neck: Supple, no JVD or lymphadenopathy. title: Cardiac Patient — Visit 2024-06-27
text: A 64-year-old male reports palpitations and dizziness over the last 48 hours. Holter monitor indicates episodes of atrial fibrillation. Patient is currently taking lisinopril 10mg. Plan to start apixaban 5mg twice daily and consult cardiology. Vitals: Temp 98.4F, HR 99, BP 143/83, RR 15, SpO2 99% on RA. General: Alert and oriented x 3, in no acute distress. HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic, PERRLA. Neck: Supple, no JVD or lymphadenopathy. Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm, normal S1 and S2, no murmurs, rubs, or gallops. Assessment and Plan: As detailed above. Will follow up in 1 weeks or sooner if symptoms worsen.

title: Cardiac Patient — Visit 2023-07-27
text: Patient evaluated for sharp, pleuritic chest pain. No history of coronary artery disease. Vitals are stable. EKG is unremarkable. Prescribed ibuprofen 600mg for suspected pericarditis. Scheduled for follow-up in one week. Vitals: Temp 98.4F, HR 93, BP 137/77, RR 15, SpO2 97% on RA. General: Alert and oriented x 3, in no acute distress. HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic, PERRLA. title: Cardiac Patient — Visit 2024-03-02
text: Patient is a 78-year-old female with known ischemic cardiomyopathy presenting for routine follow up. Reports stable angina on exertion. Continues to take aspirin 81mg, clopidogrel 75mg. Will order a stress test to evaluate exercise tolerance. Vitals: Temp 98.7F, HR 108, BP 114/88, RR 17, SpO2 99% on RA. General: Alert and oriented x 3, in no acute distress. HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic, PERRLA. Neck: Supple, no JVD or lymphadenopathy. Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm, normal S1 and S2, no murmurs, rubs, or gallops. title: Endocrine Patient — Visit 2024-11-15
text: A 56-year-old male presents for follow-up of type 2 diabetes mellitus and diabetic nephropathy. Patient reports good compliance with metformin 1000mg twice daily and jardiance 10mg daily. He denies chest pain or vision changes. Lab results show HbA1c is 7.2% and microalbuminuria is stable. Prescribed lisinopril 10mg daily for renal protection. Vitals: Temp 98.2F, HR 72, BP 128/78, RR 16, SpO2 98% on RA. General: Well-developed, alert, in no distress. HEENT: PERRLA, throat clear. Cardiovascular: RRR, no murmurs. Respiratory: Lungs clear bilaterally. Extremities: No peripheral edema. Pulses intact. Assessment: Stable Type 2 Diabetes with moderate glycemic control. Plan: Continue current regimen, recheck HbA1c in 3 months.

title: Pulmonary Consult — Visit 2024-08-20
text: A 68-year-old female with severe COPD (Gold Stage III) presents with increased sputum production and mild dyspnea. She denies fever or chills. Currently uses spiriva once daily and symbicort twice daily. SpO2 is 91% on room air. Prescribed albuterol inhaler 90mcg as needed for acute wheezing. Initiated a 5-day course of prednisone 40mg and azithromycin 250mg. Vitals: Temp 98.6F, HR 88, BP 132/80, RR 20, SpO2 91% on RA. General: Mild respiratory effort, clear speech. Cardiovascular: RRR. Respiratory: Diffuse expiratory wheezing, decreased breath sounds at bases. Plan: Scheduled follow up in 10 days; instruct patient to call if symptoms deteriorate.

title: Neurology Evaluation — Visit 2024-05-12
text: A 42-year-old female presents with recurrent throbbing headaches, unilateral, accompanied by severe nausea and photophobia, consistent with migraine headache. She reports about 4 episodes per month. Prescribed sumatriptan 50mg for acute attacks and initiated topiramate 25mg daily for migraine prophylaxis. Instructed to maintain a headache diary. Vitals: Temp 98.0F, HR 68, BP 116/72, RR 14, SpO2 99% on RA. General: Alert and oriented x 3, quiet room requested. Neurological: Cranial nerves II-XII intact, motor strength 5/5, sensation intact. Reflexes 2+ symmetrical. Plan: Return in 6 weeks to evaluate prophylactic response.

title: Cardiology Consultation — Visit 2024-09-05
text: A 71-year-old male with a history of hypertension, coronary artery disease, and coronary artery bypass graft presents for checkup. He reports occasional exertional dyspnea but denies chest pain or syncope. Patient is compliant with clopidogrel 75mg daily, carvedilol 12.5mg twice daily, and atorvastatin 80mg daily. ECG shows normal sinus rhythm with old Q waves in inferior leads. Vitals: Temp 98.4F, HR 64, BP 122/74, RR 16, SpO2 97% on RA. Cardiovascular: RRR, no murmurs. Respiratory: Clear to auscultation. Extremities: No edema. Plan: Continue current medication regimen, schedule annual echocardiogram.

title: General Practice Clinic — Visit 2024-04-18
text: A 29-year-old female presents with a 3-day history of sore throat, subjective fever, and painful swallowing. Rapid strep test is negative, ruling out streptococcal pharyngitis. Diagnosed with acute viral pharyngitis. Advised supportive care, warm saline gargles, and ibuprofen as needed for pain. Vitals: Temp 99.1F, HR 80, BP 110/70, RR 15, SpO2 98% on RA. HEENT: Erythematous posterior pharynx, tonsillar hypertrophy without exudates, tender bilateral anterior cervical lymphadenopathy. Plan: Follow up in 3-5 days if symptoms do not improve.
